summary.default_posture: HTTP/3 uses static-only QPACK by default.
summary.opt_in_posture: Dynamic QPACK field-section references, encoder/decoder instruction streams, and bounded blocked-stream scheduling are supported through H3QpackMode::DynamicTableAllowed plus QpackInstructionStreamState.
summary.parity_boundary: The native HTTP/3 state machine supports opt-in QPACK instruction-stream processing and blocked-stream wake scheduling, but this is not a claim of h3/quinn drop-in parity, full QUIC deployment parity, or default dynamic QPACK enablement.

evidence: H3Settings encode/decode preserves QPACK capacity, blocked-stream, max-field-section, DATAGRAM, Extended CONNECT, and unknown settings.
H3ConnectionState enforces duplicate SETTINGS and control-stream frame restrictions.
source_markers: duplicate SETTINGS on remote control stream

evidence: SETTINGS_H3_DATAGRAM and SETTINGS_ENABLE_CONNECT_PROTOCOL are parsed, encoded, and validated at the native HTTP/3 protocol layer.
This matrix does not claim a full QUIC DATAGRAM transport implementation beyond the native HTTP/3 frame/settings surface.
Static-only mode rejects nonzero dynamic-table capacity and blocked-stream settings.
source_markers: qpack blocked streams must be zero in static-only mode
dynamic qpack table disabled by policy

evidence: Dynamic QPACK field-section encoding and decoding are available when the caller supplies QpackContext and enables H3QpackMode::DynamicTableAllowed.
Tests cover dynamic table capacity, eviction, reference protection, required-insert-count bounds, and relative-index resolution.
QPACK string literals reuse the HPACK Huffman encoder/decoder and select Huffman when it is smaller.
Tests cover decoding Huffman strings in static mode.
Remote QPACK encoder and decoder unidirectional streams are recognized, duplicate stream creation is rejected, and the streams can be registered with QpackInstructionStreamState.
QPACK streams continue to reject HTTP/3 frame mapping; instruction bytes are processed through the explicit instruction-stream API.
source_markers: qpack streams carry instructions, not h3 frames

evidence: QpackInstructionStreamState processes encoder-stream SetDynamicTableCapacity, InsertWithNameReference, InsertWithoutNameReference, and Duplicate instructions when H3QpackMode::DynamicTableAllowed is enabled.
Decoder-stream feedback handles InsertCountIncrement, HeaderAcknowledgement, and StreamCancellation.
The proof runner emits pass rows for encoder instruction round trip, decoder feedback round trip, malformed instruction, wrong-stream instruction, and static-only rejection.
Blocked-stream SETTINGS are parsed, policy-checked, enforced as a bound, and wired into QpackInstructionStreamState.

Received field sections with Required Insert Count above Known Received Count enter a bounded blocked state and unblock deterministically after encoder instructions insert the required dynamic-table entry.
The proof runner emits pass rows for blocked-then-unblocked-stream, cancellation-while-blocked, and capacity-exceeded.

validation_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test http3_qpack_support_matrix_contract --features test-internals,http3 -- --nocapture

rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_asupersync_1xxmyo cargo test -p asupersync --lib --features test-internals,http3 qpack_instruction_stream -- --nocapture
